title: Treasury Continues to Disrupt Hamas' Sham Charity Network as the Group Refuses to Disarm
summary: The Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated four sham charities—Ghazi Destek Dernegi, Hayat Yolu, Palestinian White Hands Assistance and Solidarity Association, and Komite Nasional Untuk Rakyat Palestina—for materially supporting Hamas's military wing in violation of Executive Order 13224. The action targets Hamas's global funding network that channels money through nonprofits to Hamas-controlled charities in Gaza.
key_points: OFAC designated four sham charities under E.O. 13224 for providing material support to Hamas's military wing (Izz al-Din al-Qassam Brigades)
Three Türkiye-based entities (GDD, Hayat Yolu, Palestinian White Hands) and one Indonesia-based entity (KNRP) were designated
Internal Hamas documents captured by authorities show how funds were routed to support individual Hamas members and construction projects benefiting Hamas
Hayat Yolu also serves as an operational headquarters and financial hub for the Muslim Brotherhood
This action follows previous OFAC actions on January 21, 2026 and June 10, 2025 against Hamas-linked charities
All property and interests in property of designated persons in U.S. possession are blocked; U.S. persons are prohibited from transacting with blocked persons
why_it_matters: This is a counterterrorism sanctions action targeting terrorist financing networks. While significant for national security and geopolitical risk, it does not directly relate to financial market structure or macroeconomic policy catalysts that drive market movements.
